message.content:
Create 1 file in C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\docs\launch\CHECKLIST.md. The directory already exists. Use Write tool. Do NOT commit.

Write a master launch checklist with:

**Header:** "CitedBy.ai Launch Campaign — Master Checklist" with campaign name, duration (4 weeks), launch day target (Tuesday TBD, 12:01 AM PT).

**Week 1: Asset Creation** — Sections for Technical (deploy waitlist, run migration, test flow, set up email provider, implement welcome email), Content (finalize 3 blog posts, publish), Visual Assets (5 screenshots, demo video, demo GIF, PH thumbnail, LinkedIn carousel).

**Week 2: Content & Community** — PH Coming Soon page, schedule Twitter pre-launch, publish LinkedIn posts 1-3, post in r/SEO, join SEO communities, implement emails 2-3.

**Week 3: Warm-Up** — Schedule Twitter posts 8-14, LinkedIn posts 4-6, share waitlist in communities, reach out to 50+ PH users, send pre-launch email, implement email 4, write HN/IH/Reddit launch posts, dry run of launch day.

**Week 4: Launch Week** — Detailed launch day timeline (00:01 PT through evening) and post-launch tasks (Wed-Fri).

**KPI Targets table** with Target and Actual columns: Waitlist signups 500+, PH upvotes 200+, PH Coming Soon followers 200+, Launch day visits 2,000+, Email open rate 40%+, Waitlist→signup conversion 15-20%.

All items as checkboxes [ ].

Write the file. Do not commit.

content.text: I'll create the master launch checklist for CitedBy.ai in the specified directory.

input.content:
# CitedBy.ai Launch Campaign — Master Checklist

**Campaign:** CitedBy.ai Product Launch  
**Duration:** 4 weeks  
**Target Launch Day:** Tuesday TBD, 12:01 AM PT

---

## Week 1: Asset Creation

### Technical Setup
- [ ] Deploy waitlist landing page
- [ ] Run database migration for user schema
- [ ] Test complete waitlist signup flow end-to-end
- [ ] Set up email service provider (SendGrid/Mailgun/Brevo)
- [ ] Implement welcome email template (email #1)
- [ ] Configure email delivery webhooks and tracking
- [ ] Set up analytics on landing page (GA4/Amplitude)

### Content
- [ ] Finalize blog post #1 (problem statement)
- [ ] Finalize blog post #2 (solution deep-dive)
- [ ] Finalize blog post #3 (use cases/benefits)
- [ ] Publish all 3 blog posts to company blog
- [ ] Set up SEO meta tags and open graph images

### Visual Assets
- [ ] Create 5 product screenshots (feature highlights)
- [ ] Record product demo video (2-3 minutes)
- [ ] Create animated demo GIF
- [ ] Design Product Hunt thumbnail (600x500px)
- [ ] Design LinkedIn carousel (5 slides)
- [ ] Create Twitter header image for launch

---

## Week 2: Content & Community

### Product Hunt
- [ ] Prepare Product Hunt Coming Soon page
- [ ] Add product description and tagline
- [ ] Upload thumbnail and gallery images
- [ ] Schedule Product Hunt Coming Soon announcement

### Social Media & Communities
- [ ] Schedule 7 Twitter pre-launch posts
- [ ] Publish LinkedIn post #1 (announcement)
- [ ] Publish LinkedIn post #2 (feature highlight)
- [ ] Publish LinkedIn post #3 (use case)
- [ ] Post in r/SEO with discussion starter
- [ ] Join relevant SEO communities (Slack, Discord)
- [ ] Start discussions in 3+ community channels
- [ ] Implement email #2 (feature teaser for waitlist)
- [ ] Implement email #3 (early access offer)

### Community Engagement
- [ ] Respond to community comments and questions
- [ ] Gather feedback from early community interactions

---

## Week 3: Warm-Up

### Sustained Social Presence
- [ ] Schedule Twitter posts #8-14 (product benefits, tips, behind-the-scenes)
- [ ] Publish LinkedIn posts #4-6 (case studies, tips, team highlights)

### Community Outreach
- [ ] Share waitlist link in relevant communities
- [ ] Reach out to 50+ Product Hunt users with personalized messages
- [ ] Follow up with engaged community members
- [ ] Seed discussions in forums (HN, IH, Reddit)

### Email Marketing
- [ ] Send pre-launch email to waitlist (72 hours before launch)
- [ ] Implement email #4 (last reminder + early access incentive)

### Content Preparation
- [ ] Write Hacker News launch post (title + comments strategy)
- [ ] Write Indie Hackers launch post
- [ ] Write Reddit launch post for r/SEO and relevant subreddits
- [ ] Prepare Product Hunt launch post (title, tagline, description)

### Logistics
- [ ] Conduct full dry run of launch day timeline
- [ ] Test all email sends and confirmations
- [ ] Verify analytics tracking is working
- [ ] Prepare response templates for common questions
- [ ] Brief team on launch day responsibilities
- [ ] Set up launch day monitoring dashboard

---

## Week 4: Launch Week

### Launch Day (Tuesday, 12:01 AM PT)

**00:01 - 01:00 PT**
- [ ] Publish Product Hunt post
- [ ] Post Hacker News link
- [ ] Post Indie Hackers link
- [ ] Post Reddit links to relevant subreddits
- [ ] Send launch day email to waitlist
- [ ] Begin monitoring analytics and engagement

**01:00 - 08:00 PT**
- [ ] Actively engage on Product Hunt (respond to comments/questions)
- [ ] Monitor and respond to HN comments
- [ ] Monitor and respond to Reddit comments
- [ ] Monitor email metrics (open rate, click-through rate)
- [ ] Share launch across personal networks
- [ ] Monitor system performance and error logs

**08:00 - 12:00 PT**
- [ ] Continue community engagement across platforms
- [ ] Respond to all user inquiries and feedback
- [ ] Share user testimonials/wins
- [ ] Celebrate milestones (if reached) on social media
- [ ] Monitor conversion metrics

**12:00 - 18:00 PT**
- [ ] Post updates/behind-the-scenes on Twitter/LinkedIn
- [ ] Engage with news outlets/tech blogs
- [ ] Continue community platform engagement
- [ ] Analyze early performance data

**18:00 - 23:59 PT**
- [ ] Share end-of-day summary metrics
- [ ] Thank early supporters publicly
- [ ] Prepare day 2 content and engagement plan
- [ ] Brief team on day 1 results

### Post-Launch: Wednesday - Friday

**Wednesday (Day 2)**
- [ ] Publish follow-up blog post or case study
- [ ] Send thank you email to early adopters
- [ ] Share Product Hunt ranking updates
- [ ] Engage with press/media inquiries
- [ ] Continue social media presence
- [ ] Analyze day 1 metrics and adjust strategy

**Thursday (Day 3)**
- [ ] Publish user story/testimonial post
- [ ] Host Twitter Spaces or livestream Q&A (optional)
- [ ] Expand coverage to additional communities
- [ ] Send onboarding sequence to new signups
- [ ] Optimize landing page based on early feedback

**Friday (Day 4)**
- [ ] Compile week 1 launch report
- [ ] Share lessons learned internally
- [ ] Plan for sustainable post-launch growth
- [ ] Set up retention and activation campaigns

---

## KPI Targets

| Metric | Target | Actual |
|--------|--------|--------|
| Waitlist signups | 500+ | ___ |
| Product Hunt upvotes | 200+ | ___ |
| PH Coming Soon followers | 200+ | ___ |
| Launch day website visits | 2,000+ | ___ |
| Email open rate | 40%+ | ___ |
| Waitlist → signup conversion | 15-20% | ___ |
| Social media engagement rate | 5%+ | ___ |
| Community post reach | 10,000+ | ___ |

---

**Notes:**
- [ ] Assign owners for each section
- [ ] Set calendar reminders for all scheduled posts
- [ ] Create shared launch day monitoring dashboard
- [ ] Establish communication channels for launch day coordination
- [ ] Document all learnings post-launch for future campaigns

content.text:
Done. I've created the comprehensive CitedBy.ai Launch Campaign Master Checklist at `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\docs\launch\CHECKLIST.md`.

The checklist includes:

1. **Header** with campaign name, 4-week duration, and target launch day (Tuesday TBD, 12:01 AM PT)

2. **Week 1: Asset Creation** with three subsections:
   - Technical Setup (7 checkboxes for deploy, testing, email setup)
   - Content (5 checkboxes for blog posts)
   - Visual Assets (6 checkboxes for screenshots, videos, social assets)

3. **Week 2: Content & Community** covering:
   - Product Hunt setup
   - Social media scheduling and community engagement
   - Email implementation

4. **Week 3: Warm-Up** with:
   - Sustained Twitter/LinkedIn posting schedule
   - Community outreach and 50+ PH user outreach
   - Email campaigns and content preparation
   - Launch day dry run and logistics

5. **Week 4: Launch Week** with detailed hourly timeline from 00:01 PT through evening, plus post-launch tasks for Wed-Fri

6. **KPI Targets table** with 8 metrics including waitlist signups, PH upvotes, followers, visit counts, email metrics, and conversion rates—each with Target and Actual columns

All items are formatted as checkboxes `[ ]` throughout. The file is ready for your team to use and track progress.
